free_text: Cross sections of inelastic neutron scattering
for palladium isotope nuclei
Graphs, theoret.analysis
Data obtained via INTERNET
Graphs, experimental details
Wattecamps, Rolling,Schubert
Meister
Koning, Hogenbirk Gruppelaar
7 MV Van de Graaff accelerator.
Pulsed proton beam is focused on thick lithium
target, pulse width 1.5 ns, period 1600 ns.
Thick Li target with beryllium window, proton
energy 4.0 and 5.5 MeV. Neutron target assembly is
surrounded by a shield (barrel - 60 cm diameter, 1 m
length, and paraffin+ Li2-C-O3, axial collimation
channel is made of copper)
Time-of-Flight, flight base is 4 m
High purity Ge-detector for gammas, volume
75 cm3, resolution 2-3 keV. A sample-detector assembly
is surrounded by 7-cm lead shield and 40 cm of
paraffin Li2-C-O3 mixture. Detector covers a cone of
90 deg scattering angles
.Thin organic scintillator for measurement of
neutron spectra
Corrections were made on:
* background subtraction (30 to 80%),
* for count losses of gamma-ray selection (1 to 20%),
* neutron beam attenuation in the sample (1 to 7%),
* gamma-ray attenuation in the sample (1 to 20%),
* energy-dependent efficiency of gamma-ray detection.
.Natural palladium sample positioned at 3.87 m,
diameter is 68 mm, weight 55.4 g, natural isotope
mixture. On the back side is placed B-10 sample
B4-C disk, 70 mm diameter, 0.399 gram/cm2 of B-10
Total error
Counting statistics
Uncertainty in neutron absorption and multiple
scattering in the Pd and B samples
Uncertainty in ratio of irradiated areas
of the Pd and B samples
Uncertainty in gamma ray absorption in the
samples (B-line)
Uncertainty in gamma ray absorption in the
samples (Pd-line)
Uncertainty in conversion of the detection
efficiency ratio to the extended sample geometry
Uncertainty in the geometry transformation
for all lines
Meister, 19980813, via INTERNET;
same data published in GE/R/VG/83/94 except data
presented in Subents 7-12.
+ + Compiled by S.Maev   + +
Checking done by NEXFOR code has damaged
numerical data
Corrections made in DATA sections,
numerical data in SAN 002 - 012.
SD: Reaction to 10B(n,alpha+gamma) from
GE/R/VG/83/94 was added (see Subents 13-14).
ERR-ANALYS updated; MONITOR moved to Subents 2-12.
SD: MONITOR updated to Subents 2-12.
EN-RSL deleted (value relates to time resolution).
Reaction ratios were calibrated with the
B-10(n,alpha) cross section.  The cross section
data are taken from NSE,114,352 (see MONIT-REF) and
transformed to the energy bin structure. The numerical
values actually used are given in MONIT col.
